name: workspaces
context: Structure and manage monorepo workspaces тАФ apps/, packages/, internal packages, and workspace dependencies
info: This skill covers structuring a Turborepo monorepo: defining workspace directories (apps/ for applications, packages/ for shared libraries), configuring workspace discovery via package manager (npm/yarn workspaces in package.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml), creating internal packages with proper exports and naming conventions, managing cross-workspace dependencies, and using turbo prune to create minimal deployment subsets. Agents use this skill when bootstrapping a monorepo, adding new workspaces, restructuring packages, or setting up internal shared libraries. See [[turbo/config]] for turbo.json setup, [[turbo/pipelines]] for task orchestration across workspaces, and [[turbo/caching]] for workspace-level cache behavior.
triggers: create a new workspace in turbo
add a package to the monorepo
set up apps and packages directories
generate a new workspace with turbo gen
body:
# Workspace Management

Turborepo workspaces are the individual packages in your monorepo. Each workspace is a directory with its own package.json, source code, and scripts. Turborepo discovers workspaces through your package manager's workspace configuration.

## Directory Structure

```
my-monorepo/
тФЬтФАтФА apps/
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА web/              # Next.js app
тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА package.json
тФВ   тФВ   тФФтФАтФА src/
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА api/              # Express API
тФВ       тФЬтФАтФА package.json
тФВ       тФФтФАтФА src/
тФЬтФАтФА packages/
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА ui/               # Shared React components
тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА package.json
тФВ   тФВ   тФФтФАтФА src/
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА utils/            # Shared utilities
тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА package.json
тФВ   тФВ   тФФтФАтФА src/
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА config/           # Shared configs (tsconfig, eslint)
тФВ       тФЬтФАтФА package.json
тФВ       тФФтФАтФА src/
тФЬтФАтФА package.json          # Root тАФ declares workspaces
тФЬтФАтФА turbo.json            # Turborepo config
тФФтФАтФА pnpm-workspace.yaml   # (pnpm only)
```

## Workspace Configuration

### npm / yarn / bun

```json
{
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"],
  "devDependencies": {
    "turbo": "latest"
  },
  "packageManager": "pnpm@9.0.0"
}
```

### pnpm

```yaml
# pnpm-workspace.yaml
packages:
  - "apps/*"
  - "packages/*"
```

## Internal Package Setup

Every workspace needs a properly configured package.json:

```json
{
  "name": "@repo/ui",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "exports": {
    ".": "./src/index.ts",
    "./button": "./src/button.tsx",
    "./card": "./src/card.tsx"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts",
    "dev": "tsup src/index.ts --format esm,cjs --dts --watch",
    "lint": "eslint src/",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.0.0",
    "tsup": "^8.0.0"
  }
}
```

**Key conventions:**
- Use `@repo/` or `@org/` namespace prefixes to avoid npm registry conflicts
- Set `private: true` for internal packages not published to npm
- Use the `exports` field to define clean entrypoints
- Include `build`, `lint`, and `typecheck` scripts for [[turbo/pipelines]] to orchestrate

## Consuming Internal Packages

Add workspace dependencies in the consuming package:

```json
{
  "name": "@repo/web",
  "dependencies": {
    "@repo/ui": "workspace:*",
    "@repo/utils": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

pnpm uses `workspace:*` protocol. npm and yarn resolve workspace packages by name matching.

Import in code:
```typescript
import { Button } from "@repo/ui/button";
import { formatDate } from "@repo/utils";
```

## Adding a New Workspace

```bash
# Manual approach
mkdir -p packages/new-lib/src
cd packages/new-lib
npm init -y  # or pnpm init

# Using turbo gen
turbo gen workspace
```

After creating the workspace, install dependencies from the root to update the lockfile:
```bash
pnpm install  # or npm install / yarn
```

## turbo prune

Create a minimal monorepo subset for deployment:

```bash
# Create sparse monorepo with only web and its dependencies
turbo prune web

# Output structure:
# out/
#   json/       тАФ pruned package.json files
#   full/       тАФ full source code
#   pnpm-lock.yaml (or equivalent)
```

This is useful for Docker builds тАФ copy only the packages needed for a specific app:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Prune to get only what web needs
COPY . .
RUN npx turbo prune web --docker

# Install pruned dependencies
FROM node:20-alpine AS installer
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/out/json/ .
COPY --from=builder /app/out/pnpm-lock.yaml ./pnpm-lock.yaml
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# Build
COPY --from=builder /app/out/full/ .
RUN pnpm turbo build --filter=web
```

## Constraints

- **No nested workspace globs**: `apps/**` is not supported тАФ use `apps/*` and `apps/group/*`
- **No cross-package relative imports**: Never use `../` paths тАФ install packages as dependencies
- **Every workspace needs package.json**: Directories without package.json are not workspaces
- **Workspace names must be unique**: The `name` field in package.json must be unique across the monorepo

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `TURBO_FILTER` | Default --filter value |

## Filtering Workspaces

```bash
turbo build --filter=web            # By package name
turbo build --filter=@repo/ui       # By scoped name
turbo build --filter={./apps/*}     # By directory pattern
turbo build --filter=web...         # Package and its dependencies
turbo build --filter=...web         # Package and its dependents
turbo build --filter=[HEAD^1]       # Changed since last commit
turbo build --filter=web --filter=api  # Multiple filters
```

sections.title: Monorepo directory structure
sections.body: The standard Turborepo layout uses `apps/` for deployable applications and `packages/` for shared libraries. Each directory under these globs is a workspace with its own package.json. The root contains turbo.json, the root package.json (with `private: true` and workspace config), and the lockfile. For pnpm, workspaces are declared in pnpm-workspace.yaml; for npm/yarn/bun, in the root package.json `workspaces` array.

sections.title: Creating internal packages
sections.body: Internal packages use `@repo/` or `@org/` namespaces, set `private: true`, and define clean entrypoints via the `exports` field. Consumers add them as dependencies with `workspace:*` (pnpm) or by name (npm/yarn). The `exports` field maps import paths to source files: `".": "./src/index.ts"`, `"./button": "./src/button.tsx"`. Include build, lint, and typecheck scripts for Turborepo orchestration.

sections.title: Pruning for deployment
sections.body: Use `turbo prune <workspace>` to generate a minimal monorepo with only the target workspace and its dependencies. The `--docker` flag generates a Docker-optimized layout with `out/json/` (package.json files for dependency install layer) and `out/full/` (full source for build layer). This dramatically reduces Docker image size and build time for monorepo deployments.

sections.title: Workspace filtering
sections.body: The `--filter` flag targets specific workspaces for task execution. Filter by name (`--filter=web`), scoped name (`--filter=@repo/ui`), directory pattern (`--filter={./apps/*}`), dependency graph (`--filter=web...` for dependencies, `--filter=...web` for dependents), or git changes (`--filter=[HEAD^1]`). Combine multiple filters. Use `!` to negate: `--filter=!web`. The `--affected` flag auto-filters to packages changed on the current branch.

sections.title: Generating workspaces
sections.body: Use `turbo gen workspace` to scaffold a new workspace interactively. It creates the directory, package.json, and initial files. Custom generators can be defined in the `turbo/generators` directory at the repo root using Plop templates. This standardizes workspace creation across teams.

related.note: Workspace patterns must be configured in package manager config and turbo.json
Tasks run across workspaces based on dependency graph
TypeScript project references mirror workspace dependencies

scripts.name: Manage Workspaces
scripts.description: Create new workspaces, list existing ones, or validate workspace configuration

pruneTarget.description: Workspace to prune for (for prune action)

workspaces.description: List of workspace names (for list action)

depends.reason: turbo.json must exist for workspace orchestration

author: dojo-community
